questions.subject: Biology

questions.text: Mutualism is a type of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mutualism is a type of symbiotic relationship where all species involved benefit from their interactions.

Commensalism, in biology, a relationship between individuals of two species in which one species obtains food or other benefits from the other without either harming or benefiting the latter. commensalism. Category: Animals & Nature. Related Topics: mimicry host inquilinism interspecific association.

Parasitism is generally defined as a relationship between the two living species in which one organism is benefitted at the expense of the other. The organism that is benefitted is called the parasite, while the one that is harmed is called the host. A few examples of parasites are tapeworms, fleas, and barnacles.

Predation is a biological interaction where one organism, the predator, kills and eats another organism, its prey. It is one of a family of common feeding behaviours that includes parasitism and micropredation (which usually do not kill the host) and parasitoidism (which always does, eventually).

questions.explanation: Mutualism is a type of symbiotic relationship where all species involved benefit from their interactions.

questions.text: The leaves with a very small surface area, are found in
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In this type the surface area of leaves is very large to transpire water excessively. Extensive stomata are present on the upper surface facing the atmosphere to promote loss of water

Mesophytes have moderate water availability. In suicient supply of water stomata are kept open to promote loss of excess water, however, in restricted supply stomata close to prevent the loss e.g. Brassica, rose, mango etc.
Many xerophytes possess small, thick leaves to limit water loss by reducing surface area proportional to the volume.
Sciophytes are plants that grow in the shade and represent the understory in forest stratification. For example, java moss and club moss.

questions.text: The compound which takes part in the urea cycle is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Two ammonia and one carbon dioxide molecule are shunted into the cycle to generate one molecule of urea. One ammonia molecule combines with carbon dioxide and an already available precursor from the previous cycle ornithine to form citrulline, subsequently, another ammonia combines to form arginine. The arginine is split by arginase to form urea and the precursor ornithine for next cycle.

questions.text: Osteomalacia includes a number of disorders in which bones receive inadequate
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Osteomalacia, often referred to as "soft bone disease," is a metabolic bone disorder characterized by the inadequate mineralization of bone tissue. This condition results from a deficiency in vitamin D, calcium, or phosphate, weakening and softening bones.

questions.text: Luteinizing hormone in human females induces
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In women, ovulation of mature follicles on the ovary is induced by a large burst of LH secretion - the preovulatory LH surge. Residual cells within ovulated follicles proliferate to form corpora lutea, which secrete the steroid hormones - progesterone and estradiol.

questions.text: The branch of biology which deals with the study of abnormal development is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Morphology is the study of the internal structure of words and forms a core part of linguistic study today. The term morphology is Greek and is a makeup of morph- meaning 'shape, form', and -ology which means 'the study of something'.
Embryology, the study of the formation and development of an embryo and fetus.
Teratology – the study of abnormalities of physiological development.
A subspecialty of obstetrics concerned with the care of the fetus and complicated, high-risk pregnancies. Perinatology is also known as maternal-fetal medicine.

questions.explanation: Teratology – the study of abnormalities of physiological development.

questions.text: In turner syndrome the affected person has a set of chromosomes
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: To diagnose Turner syndrome, doctors use a special blood test that looks at chromosomes, called a karyotype test (chromosomal analysis). Results that indicate TS show only one X chromosome instead of two X chromosomes, with a total of 45 chromosomes instead of the usual 46.
